title: [firtool] Run HWCleanup pass again after canonicalizer
body:
`./bin/firtool ../perf/regress/chipyard.TestHarness.RocketSmall1Medium1Big1_BoomMedium1Large1Mega1.top.v.hi.fir` now generates many like this (for example in always block of `TLMonitor` module):
```sv
    automatic logic _T_397 = _T_217 & ~(|a_first_counter);      // Edges.scala:229:28, :230:25, Monitor.scala:396:20

    if (_T_397) // Monitor.scala:397:15
      opcode <= io_in_a_bits_opcode;    // Monitor.scala:397:15
    if (_T_397) // Monitor.scala:398:15
      param <= io_in_a_bits_param;      // Monitor.scala:398:15
    if (_T_397) // Monitor.scala:399:15
      size <= io_in_a_bits_size;        // Monitor.scala:399:15
    if (_T_397) // Monitor.scala:400:15
      source <= io_in_a_bits_source;    // Monitor.scala:400:15
    if (_T_397) // Monitor.scala:401:15
      address <= io_in_a_bits_address;
```
We expect these if statements are merged into one statement but they are not. This is because if statements are created by `PAssignOp` canonicalizer (note that  HWCleanup is executed before canonicalization).

https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/4e20f1ad18eda5b6d03f9f96e5f0fc08a15a12a6/lib/Dialect/SV/SVOps.cpp#L1272-L1274

If we run HWCleanup again after canonicalization, we can merge them into one if statement: 
```sv
if (_T_217 & ~(|a_first_counter)) begin     // Edges.scala:229:28, :230:25, Monitor.scala:396:20, :401:15
      opcode <= io_in_a_bits_opcode;    // Monitor.scala:397:15
      param <= io_in_a_bits_param;      // Monitor.scala:398:15
      size <= io_in_a_bits_size;        // Monitor.scala:399:15
      source <= io_in_a_bits_source;    // Monitor.scala:400:15
      address <= io_in_a_bits_address;  // Monitor.scala:401:15
 end
```

I think HWCleanup is cheaper enough compared to other passes, so I would like to suggest to add HWCleanup pass to the later pipeline. WDYT?
